title: Quick Player Trade
description: A server-side mod offering a simple way to give and receive items between players : by right clicking on them!
body:
![Quick Player Trade Banner](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/OcO4rWRt/images/9fb1dd8515976157249c36dc9b9d7f8b70f47c51.png)

#### ⚠️ This mod is exclusive to Fabric. There is/will be **no Forge version**. Take a look at the FAQ below for more information.

## ❤ Description ❤

**Quick Player Trade** is a ***server-side*** mod offering a simple way to give and receive items between players.
It aims to offer a more ***polite and fluid experience*** when ***handing out items to your friends and foes***, instead of throwing various blocks and other pixelated bits and pieces to them.

> Where did you drop it?  
> Did you give it to me?  
> Wait, it came back in my inventory, one more time

***Ever had one of these situations?*** *No more with* ***Quick Player Trade***!

## ❤ Features ❤

### Configuration options

- As a server administrator, you can configure a blacklist of items you would like the mod to ignore. This allows to still be able to "fish" players with a fishing rod (why not?) or still being able to eat face to face at a table with petals and candles.
- As a player, you can disable the behavior of the mod with the command `/qpt toggle`. This can be useful for combat purposes, or if you simply aren't enthusiastic about my wonderful, amazing, splendid mod. No hard feeling!

### Giving items

- While standing, right clicking a player will give them one item from the stack you are holding in your left hand.
- While sneaking, right clicking a player will give them the entire stack you are holding in your hand.
- Quick Player Trade makes sure your item are given with the same Nbt tags, meaning the custom names, enchantments and other information that mods could change are kept as is.
- A feedback message is sent to the giver and the receiver to notify them of the gift, just like a lovely little birthday card!

## Support me

> Feel free to check out my other mod, [Stendhal: a client-side Fabric mod offering new and enhanced visual utilities for books, signs, anvil and the in-game chat.](https://modrinth.com/mod/stendhal)

<details>
<summary>❤ FAQ ❤</summary>

Q: Should the mod be installed on the client in order to work?  
A: No! This mod is entirely server-side, meaning any vanilla/Fabric client will be able to use it when connecting to the said server. As long as QPT is installed on it.

Q: Can I use your mod in/for my public modpack/datapack/mod compilation/server-side mod list?  
A: Please do! A credit would be appreciated in your modlist.  
Simply do not appropriate yourself this mod.

Q: Will this mod ever come to Forge?  
A: No! This mod is intended to push a little forward the Fabric community, and this is my choice to explicitly not make it available to another mod loader for the time being.

Q: Where can I find the source code of the project?  
A: As of now, nowhere. I don't exclude opening the source code later down the line! It is just not my habit, and I keep my mod very much up to date.

Q: Are you the developer of the mod named "Stendhal"?  
A: Glad you spotted it! [Check out Stendhal here.](https://modrinth.com/mod/stendhal)

</details>

## ❤ Upcoming Updates ❤

Quick Player Trade is being actively developed and ported to newer Minecraft versions over time.

> If you would like to give suggestion and follow the development more actively, please join [my Discord Community]

## ❤ Community ❤

You can join me on [my Discord Community] to discuss about Quick Player Trade alongside my various other projects, whether they are linked to Minecraft modding or not!

If you would like, feel free to support me on [Ko-Fi], it would make me really happy and motivated!
